message:
Fix BeadsView script polling effect to run once on mount

The useEffect that polls for window.initSqlJs, window.d3, and
window.ForceGraph globals had [sqlLoaded, d3Loaded, forceGraphLoaded]
in its dependency array while also calling setSqlLoaded, setD3Loaded,
and setForceGraphLoaded inside the interval callback. This caused
the effect to tear down and re-create the setInterval every time
any of those state variables changed—the interval would fire, set
a state value, trigger a re-render, which changed the dependency
array, which destroyed the old interval and created a new one.

While the guards (e.g., !sqlLoaded before setSqlLoaded(true))
prevented true infinite state updates, the unnecessary effect
re-runs wasted cycles and created brief windows where no interval
was active (between cleanup and re-creation).

Fix: change dependency array to [] so the polling interval is
created once on mount and cleaned up once on unmount. The interval
already self-clears when all three globals are detected, so no
external dependency tracking is needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
